kind: algorithmic complexity-class replacement (O(n^2) -> O(n log n))
lever_summary: Replace the direct O(n^2) DFT fallback for non-power-of-two FFT lengths with Bluestein's chirp-z transform: the length-n DFT becomes a length-m=next_pow2(2n-1) convolution evaluated with the existing radix-2 power-of-two FFT, giving O(n log n). Power-of-two lengths keep Cooley-Tukey unchanged.
method: same-bench A/B on RCH Criterion; baseline = git-stashed fft.rs (O(n^2) dft_1d) with the new non-pow2 benches present; candidate = Bluestein.

change.lever: Added bluestein_dft_into (chirp-z via radix2_fft_1d_into); fft_1d_into/ifft_1d_into route non-power-of-two to it. dft_1d/idft_1d/precompute_twiddles became #[cfg(test)] DFT references; removed the now-unused test-only radix2_fft_1d. Added eval/fft_1000_complex128 and eval/fft_1009_prime_complex128 benches.

parity.type: tolerance-parity-revalidation (FFT is inherently floating-tolerance, not bit-exact); Bluestein != old direct-DFT bits but computes the same transform
validations: bluestein_matches_direct_dft_across_sizes: Bluestein vs O(n^2) dft_1d/idft_1d at 1e-9 across n in {3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,15,17,100,257,1000}, forward+inverse+roundtrip
fj-conformance linalg_fft_oracle_parity (real jnp.fft captures): passes -> matches JAX within oracle tolerance

validation.name: fft_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --release fft

validation.name: jax_fft_oracle
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --release --test linalg_fft_oracle_parity

validation.name: clippy
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

note: Genuine different-complexity-class win per the NO-CEILING addendum (not a micro-lever). 100% safe Rust on the existing radix-2 kernel; no C/FFTW/MKL. Next: batched FFT (eval_fft applies fft_1d_into per row over the last axis) could reuse Bluestein scratch (a/b/fa/fb/prod/conv) across rows to cut per-row allocation.
